prerequisites: A Webflow account with a published site you want to extract data from. The Data API operates on Webflow sites and their CMS, forms, and e-commerce data.

A Webflow API token (a bearer token). Use a Site Token for single-site access, or a Workspace Token for read-only access across multiple sites in a Workspace. Only a site administrator can create a site token.
The site_id of the target Webflow site. Retrieve it from the id field returned by the GET /v2/sites endpoint (or list sites with an SDK).
Token scopes that cover the data you intend to read. Scopes are chosen when the token is generated (for example sites:read, cms:read, forms:read, ecommerce:read).

authMethods.name: API token (Bearer)
authMethods.summary: Provide a Webflow API token in the api_key field. The token is sent as an HTTP bearer credential in the Authorization header (authorization: Bearer <token>) on every Data API request. Both a Site Token (single site) and a Workspace Token (read-only, multiple sites) are accepted, since both are bearer tokens. Tokens carry the scopes and permissions selected when they were generated.

setupSteps.title: Generate a Webflow API token
setupSteps.body: To create a site token, open the site in the Webflow Dashboard, go to its settings, select Apps & integrations, scroll to the API access section, and click Generate API token. Enter a name, choose the scopes the integration needs, and click Generate token. Copy and store the token securely, it is shown only once. Alternatively, generate a Workspace token from the Webflow Dashboard under Apps & Integrations > Manage > Workspace API access > Generate API token for read-only access across multiple sites.

setupSteps.title: Find your site_id
setupSteps.body: Call the list sites endpoint, GET https://api.webflow.com/v2/sites, authenticated with your token (header authorization: Bearer <token>). The response contains a sites array; copy the id value of the target site (a hex string such as 580e63e98c9a982ac9b8b741). This id is the value for the required site_id config field.

setupSteps.title: Configure the connector credentials
setupSteps.body: Set api_key to the token you generated and set site_id to the site identifier you copied. Both fields are required. The token is sent as authorization: Bearer <api_key> on each request and the connector reads data only for the specified site_id.

setupSteps.title: Set the API version (optional)
setupSteps.body: Leave accept_version at its default unless you need to pin a specific Data API version. The current production Data API is v2 (2.0.0); versioning is explicit and also reflected in the /v2/ endpoint path. Only override accept_version if you must request a particular version explicitly.
